design_philosophy.core_loop: Turn-based strategic galaxy conquest interspersed with arcade-style real-time ship duels; players colonize planets for resources, then engage enemy ships in combat when fleets meet
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Risk-reward between aggressive expansion (claiming planets) and fleet management (repairing ships at colonies); ship selection creates matchup metagame where weaker ships can win in skilled hands
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Command an alien faction across a galaxy war, making strategic decisions about fleet deployment while mastering individual ship combat through mechanical skill
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Hybrid action-strategy design that refuses to pick between real-time twitch skill and turn-based tactical thinking; emphasizes asymmetrical ship balance where no single craft dominates—victory depends on player expertise and matchup knowledge rather than unit stats alone

difficulty.checkpointing: Turn-based strategy missions allow players to restart individual combat encounters; AI can be assigned to handle strategy or combat sections for player convenience
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: High agency: players choose between factions with different ship rosters, can select which ships to field in combat, can delegate strategy/combat to AI if desired, and can practice one-on-one battles separately from campaign

progression.upgrade_system: Ship crew replenishment at captured colonies serves as healing/resource system; strategic resource generation (currency, crew) tied to planet colonization and fortification
progression.unlock_mechanism: Campaign scenarios unlock sequentially (nine missions on home computers, fifteen on Sega Genesis); all 14 ships available to both factions from start, emphasizing player choice over unlocks
replayability_hooks: Two distinct factions with different ship rosters and strategic starting positions
Ship matchup discovery encourages replaying battles with different unit combinations
Head-to-head multiplayer mode for competitive engagement
Practice mode separates combat mastery from strategic campaign
Multiple selectable scenarios allow varied campaign approaches

world_building_techniques: Instruction manual exposition of overarching war narrative between Alliance of Free Stars vs Ur-Quan Hierarchy
Campaign scenario progression reveals plot through sequential missions
Distinct alien character designs for each cockpit animation provide personality and worldbuilding flavor
Unique sound design per ship (Chenjesu drones barking, Ur-Quan Dreadnaught bellowing 'launch fighters') reinforces alien faction identity
Colorful visual design and artwork creates memorable, distinct species
Two opposing factions each with seven alien starships establishes rich interstellar political/military context

faction_indicators: Seven distinct alien species per faction with unique cultural/military identities
danger_signals: Ur-Quan invasion as existential threat to galaxy
Asymmetrical power dynamic between factions drives campaign motivation
environmental_storytelling: Rotating star map shows territorial control and expansion progress
Planet colonization mechanics embed resource scarcity and strategic territory value
Gravity well physics and planetary obstacles in combat reflect astronomical setting authenticity
Cockpit animations and unique character designs per alien provide visual worldbuilding without dialogue
